Welcome, Seb here

Home Writings

Sebestyén Gergely

Hello, nice to see you 👋    We can connect on

LinkedIn GitHub Upwork

I'm a data engineer & analyst convinced that working with tech should feel intuitive.

There's plenty happening at the intersection of Data x Computing. My page follows those developments and asks where we're heading next. I've found our profession often struggles to proactively anticipate change. It's an interesting problem to design systems that perform well today, but remain flexible enough to adapt if the better solution drops tomorrow.

datolya is the Hungarian word for the date fruit. There used to be a social network site where you could raise camels and feed them dates to give them superpowers. I thought of it as a fitting metaphor for what data allows us to do.

My background is in finance and central banking. I've also spent years supporting large-scale Tech acquisitions and building the data integration for payments systems. In my experience, working cleanly with the leading commerce providers (e.g. Apple, Google, Stripe, Adyen) goes a long way for a healthy data culture.  

I've done a lot of other things as well. You can check out some of them here:

Writings

Why Your Big Data Architecture Is Flawed Many assume that Scala outperforms pySpark

Why FX Loans Upset Eastern Europe
Strategies to eliminate post-2008 FX loan portfolios

Why Do Currencies Collapse
How to keep housing bubbles under control

What Labour Market Shocks Teach Us
How to accommodate 3,5 million arrivals

How To Plot Shifting Public Opinion
Politics has long been a race for accurate data

What Network Analyses Tell Us
Do cross-country ties predict election results
